name: Spicy Chocolate Pumpkin Mini-Cakes
recipeIngredient: 1/3 teaspoon each red & yellow liquid food color
6 Pepperidge Farm Pirouette cookies, For garnish
recipeInstructions:
Hot oven to 350°F. Lightly grease twelve 1-cup capacity mini-bundt pans. (If you only have one pan with six wells, the batter can remain at room temperature while the first batch bakes.) You can also use regular 1/2-cup capacity muffin tins using 1/3 cup batter per well.
For batter: In a large bowl with an electric mixer on medium-low speed, beat butter, sugar, cocoa, pie spice, baking powder, and baking soda until creamy. Gradually increase speed to high. Add eggs, one at a time, and beat until the mixture is fluffy. On low speed, beat in pumpkin, milk, and vanilla (the mixture will look curdled). Gradually add flour, beating just until blended. Fill each prepared well with 2/3 cup batter (if using separate tins). Smooth tops.
Bake 18 to 20 minutes until a pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool in pans on a wire rack for 10 minutes. Invert pans on racks and tap to release cakes. Let cool completely.
For frosting: In a medium saucepan, bring brown sugar, butter, and milk to a gentle boil. Stir until butter melts, sugar dissolves, and the mixture is smooth. Gradually beat confectioner's sugar and vanilla into hot mixture. Remove from heat and beat in red and yellow food coloring until frosting is pumpkin orange. (You may need to add some more food coloring.) Spoon frosting over cakes so it runs down sides, letting some cake show near the bottom. If frosting becomes too thick, stir over low heat or transfer to a microwave-safe bowl and microwave on medium (defrost) until the right consistency.
Cut wafer cookies in half crosswise and insert them in the center hole of cakes for "stems". Cover with plastic wrap and store at room temperature up to 3 days.
